sutta: now the verbal punia paid a visit to the exhorted 1 and sat down at 1 side and said lord what is the cause what is the reason why sometimes it occurs to the tatargata to preach dhamma and sometimes it does not suppose punya a monk is a believer but does not visit him then it does not occur to the tatargata to preach dhamma but when among is a believer and visits the tatargata then it occurs to him to preach dharma or among is a believer and visits the tatargata but does not sit down to listen then it does not occur to the tatargata to preach dharma or he sits down to listen but does not question that or he questions the tata gata but does not listen to dhamma with attentive ear or he listens with attentive ear but when he has heard it does not bear dhamma in mind or he bears what he has heard in mind but does not examine the truth of the doctrines he has heard or he examines the truth of the doctrines but walks not in conformity with dhamma though knowing both letter and spirit then it does not occur to the tatar gather to preach dhamma but when punia among is a believer and visits the tita sits down to listen questions the tata gaata listens with attentive ear bears what he has heard in mind examines the truth of the doctrines heard and knowing both the letter and the spirit walks in conformity with dhamma then it occurs to the tata to preach dhamma and these 8 conditions come to pass punya then it most occurs to the tata to preach dhamma that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so from this we find that sometimes people come to visit the buddha sometimes he preaches dharma to them and sometimes he does not and these are the reasons he give sometimes that person is not inclined to listen or sometimes they listen but does not question the buddha anymore or he questions but does not listen with attentive ear etc and the buddha [Music] the buddha taught now that it is we should have reverence of the dhamma and a lay person especially has to show respect and be desiring to hear the dhamma before the dhamma is taught then we find in the vinaya rules the buddha laid down that if early persons if a lay person sits higher than among he is not to be taught the dhamma or he wears a head or he claps the knees grasp the knees or he carries a weapon etc then the monk is not supposed to teach that person the dhamma in other words the dhamma is something that is invaluable and so we should show respect for it before it will be taught to us the nexus

items: bears what he has heard in mind
examines the truth of the doctrines heard
